commit.message:
fix(soulmate-pack): stop heartbeat spam + tune for real-partner cadence (#95)

* fix(soulmate-pack): persist next_send_at so heartbeat gate actually fires

should_message.py has always read data/.last_proactive_sent but never
written it — the state gate has been a dead read since the file first
landed in #1. In every branch that returns send: True, the freshly
computed next_send_at is emitted in the JSON output and then discarded.

The bug was latent until #92 because sm-onboarding previously skipped
setting heartbeat 'every' when platform routing config was already
present, so the cron had no tick schedule. Once #92 switched Step 6b
to setdefault-per-key merge, 'every' got set, ticks started firing —
and with no persisted next_send_at, every tick hits the first_run
branch and sends a proactive message.

Fix: add _persist_next_send() that mkdirs data_dir and writes
.last_proactive_sent alongside every non-force send: True branch.
--force stays stateless by design. Verified locally:
  run 1 → {"send": true, "reason": "first_run"}
  run 2 → {"send": false, "reason": "not_yet", "remaining_min": 143}

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* rename state file .last_proactive_sent → .next_send.json

The old name suggests a timestamp of something that already happened,
but the file stores a forward-looking schedule — when the next
proactive tick should fire. Rename to match actual content. Since
no earlier commit successfully wrote the file, there is no on-disk
data to migrate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* harden get_session_updated_at: surface failures, tighten timeout

The active-session gate called openclaw sessions with timeout=30s and a
blanket except Exception: pass — any failure (timeout, missing binary,
non-zero exit, JSON parse error, polluted stdout) was silently swallowed,
returning None. Callers interpret None as "no active session" and proceed
to send, so every mode of failure degraded to "heartbeat ignores the
cooldown gate" without any signal.

- timeout=30 → timeout=5 (local command, no reason to wait longer)
- subprocess exceptions logged to stderr with the underlying error
- non-zero returncode logged to stderr (was silently accepted)
- JSON parse errors logged to stderr (were silently swallowed)
- fail-open semantics preserved — on failure, gate is skipped rather
  than permanently deferring, so a broken openclaw does not silence
  heartbeat entirely. Operators see stderr logs in cron output.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* HEARTBEAT.md: require silent execution, no status narration

Users reported seeing lines like "✅ SOUL.md has Persona / ✅
Streak-check ran / ✅ should_message.py returned send: true / ✅
Generated voice message / ..." in the conversation. HEARTBEAT.md
never asked for them — the agent was narrating progress per
default LLM habit.

Make it explicit: heartbeat is internal, only step 5's in-character
message reaches the user, all other steps and exit paths end silently.
Updated both top-level HEARTBEAT.md and the template that /init
restores.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* HEARTBEAT.md: route action_type → skill, mandate reading SKILL.md first

Production logs (zooclaw) show heartbeat selfies look nothing like the
configured companion — the agent calls nano-banana-pro directly, without
--images portrait.png, generating a generic stranger each tick. It only
reads sm-photo/SKILL.md belatedly, after already having generated.

Root cause: step 5 said "execute action_type in persona" with no routing
instruction. message/question it handled fine as text, but for selfie /
voice_message / video it free-formed tool choice instead of invoking the
owning skill.

Fix: explicit action_type → skill routing table, with an up-front rule
to read the target SKILL.md before executing (matches the /selfie
convention already in AGENTS.md, now also required on heartbeat path).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* simplify: extract _warn helper, trim HEARTBEAT.md callout

Code review pass:
- Three stderr prints in get_session_updated_at shared the
  "[should_message] " prefix. Extract _warn(msg) to stop repeating
  it and make renames trivial.
- HEARTBEAT.md step 5 had a post-mortem parenthetical describing
  the nano-banana-pro incident. Trim to the rule itself; the
  selfie bullet now carries the concrete argument requirement
  (--images portrait.png|fullbody.png) inline.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* HEARTBEAT.md: tighten prose (every tick loads this file)

Drop redundant phrasing without losing meaning:
- "Silent execution" → "Silent"; "no ... no ... no ..." → single list
- Drop "Any exit path ..." sentence; "Only step 5's ... reaches the
  user" already implies it
- Drop "(uses SM_DATA_DIR or `data/`)" on step 3 — script-internal
  detail, not actionable for the agent
- Drop "route by action_type"; the bullets are self-evidently the
  routing table
- "compose text" → "text"; "Then run" → "Then"; drop "intimacy"
  prefix on --delta-type (context made clear by AGENTS.md reference)

~30% fewer words, same directives. Applied to top-level and template.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* HEARTBEAT.md: use uv run for should_message.py

Matches the canonical `uv run --python 3.12 ...` pattern used throughout
AGENTS.md — consistent Python invocation across the pack.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* HEARTBEAT.md: drop redundant selfie --images note

sm-photo's SKILL.md already mandates passing portrait/fullbody as
--images. The "read target skill's SKILL.md first" directive covers it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* HEARTBEAT.md: drop title and 'Internal only' preface

Title is redundant with filename; "Internal only —" is covered by
"Silent" + the example list. Silence rule and SKILL.md-first safety
rail preserved.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* HEARTBEAT.md: tighten silence rule to one absolute line

Replace "Silent — no narration/status/✅/HEARTBEAT_OK" enumeration with
"No chat output except step 5's in-character message." — stronger
blanket constraint, half the tokens, no loopholes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* harden state persistence: atomic write, fail-closed on corrupt, session gate on first_run

Review caught three related bugs in the recently-added persistence layer:

1. Non-atomic write: Path.write_text() can leave a torn file on crash
   or overlapping tick. Switch to tmp-sibling + os.replace().

2. Corrupt vs missing conflation: _read_json returned {} on both, so
   a torn write re-entered first_run and duplicate-sent. Introduce
   _load_next_send_at() that distinguishes the two via sentinels;
   fail closed on parse error (next scheduled write heals the file).

3. first_run bypassed the active-session gate — a missing state file
   (fresh install, clean.py, disk issue) could fire mid-conversation.
   Consolidate the gate to run on both first_run and timer-expired.

Smoke-tested: first_run, not_yet, corrupt JSON, empty {}, no .tmp leak.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* self-heal corrupt state file so heartbeat doesn't get permanently stuck

Round-2 review caught: when _load_next_send_at returns _STATE_CORRUPT,
we fail-closed but never rewrite — subsequent ticks keep reading the
same corrupt file and stay silent forever.

Remove the corrupt file on detection so the next tick sees _STATE_MISSING
and proceeds through the normal first_run path. Still fail-closed this
tick to avoid duplicate-send.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* tune heartbeat for real-partner cadence and cheaper texts

Three coordinated changes:

1. FREQ_INTERVAL retuned — default 'regular' now ~3-4/day (was 5-6/day).
   Real partners text at day-rhythm anchors, not every 2 hours.
   Simulated: gentle 2/day, regular 3.3/day, attentive 5.1/day.

2. Action weights rebalanced toward text — 40/25/15/15/5 (was 25/20/30/20/5).
   Media (voice/selfie/video) drops from 55% to 35% of sends. Real
   partners text more than they voice/photo; media stays meaningful
   and less costly overall.

3. HEARTBEAT.md compacted ~33% — merged exit-early steps into the
   should_message.py line and inlined the skill-routing table onto
   one line. Same semantics, every 30-min cron tick loads less.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* weight actions by cost: voice > selfie, video stays rare

Voice generation is cheaper than image gen; video is the most expensive.
Shift 5% from selfie → voice so the action mix tracks cost:

  message 0.40 / question 0.25 / voice 0.20 / selfie 0.10 / video 0.05

Text remains dominant (65%), voice doubles selfie, video unchanged at 5%.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Ning Hu <ning@gensmo.ai>

commit.message:
soulmate-pack: simplify, fix stale code, align docs to code (#92)

* soulmate-pack: simplify, fix stale code, align docs to code

Markdown (loaded into agent context every turn): trimmed ~26% — removed
duplicated rules across AGENTS.md/SOUL.md/skills, dropped rationale-only
examples, compacted sm-onboarding (160→112 lines). Behavior-preserving.

Python: dropped dead code (compute_streak_status, DECAY_SCHEDULE), merged
duplicated config reads, unified EN/ZH dashboard output, added mutation-
tracking so no-op heartbeats no longer rewrite intimacy.json. Total
~200 lines removed across intimacy.py, should_message.py, health_check.py.

Fixes:
- sm-relationship/SKILL.md threshold table now matches code
  [0, 200, 600, 1500, 3000, 5500, 9000, 14000] (previously diverged).
- health_check.py no longer checks three paths that never existed
  (xai_image.py, xai_video.py, sm-image-search skill).
- .agents/skills/sm-onboarding/templates/ resynced to top-level (drift
  would have restored stale versions on /init).
- should_message.py: time_weight_for was present but never called; wired
  into compute_next_send_at so proactive messages bias toward evenings
  (weight 1.3) and mornings (1.2). Night hours return 1.0 so the existing
  quiet-hours branch owns deferral to next 08:00.

Reviewed by Claude + Codex; one critical bug (night weight 0.05 bypassed
quiet-hours snap → multi-day delivery gaps) caught and fixed pre-merge.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* health_check: resolve skills under .agents/skills/ after refactor

The skill-existence check in check_workspace() was still joining
WORKSPACE / "skills" / skill, which no longer exists after #90 moved
pack skills under .agents/skills/. Also updated required_dirs to list
.agents/skills instead of the missing top-level skills/.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* sm-onboarding: merge heartbeat config instead of skipping when one exists

Platform backend (ecap-workspace #770) now injects per-agent heartbeat
delivery config on every deployment — {target: mattermost, accountId, to}
for routing. sm-onboarding's old "skip if heartbeat exists" path meant
every, directPolicy, and lightContext were never set, so the companion
had a delivery address but no tick schedule.

Switch Step 6b semantics from "set if missing" to setdefault-per-key
merge. Drop 'target: last' — the platform's 'mattermost' target is what
actually delivers to the user.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* nits from review: drop unused import, update stale comment, fail-only on shipped dirs

- intimacy.py: drop unused 'import sys' left over from earlier refactor.
- health_check.py: API-keys comment referenced removed xai_image/xai_video
  scripts; updated to reference the external designer + video-generator
  skills that actually consume these keys.
- health_check.py: split required_dirs into shipped_dirs (.agents/skills,
  scripts) and runtime_dirs (data, artifacts, memory). AUTO_FIX now only
  creates runtime dirs; a missing .agents/skills indicates a broken install
  and should fail loudly rather than be silently 'fixed' with an empty dir.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* health_check: probe video-generator skill instead of a fake pass

Previous commit replaced the stale xai_video.py check with a hardcoded
ok() that always passed. That masks a real failure mode: if video-generator
isn't installed, sm-video breaks but health_check reports green. Mirror
check_image_gen() and probe the skill via openclaw CLI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
agent-studio: explicit cron + heartbeat delivery policy (#91)

* agent-studio: explicit cron delivery fields + silent session-context capture

Align Agent Studio's cron-job documentation and code generators with the
ZooClaw Cron Job Delivery Policy (openclaw-docker#39). The old
`{"mode":"announce","channel":"last"}` default relies on
`inferDeliveryFromSessionKey`, which fails to extract `accountId` and
silently misdelivers cron messages (or drops them entirely).

**Delivery must be explicit:**
- `agentId` — from the ZooClaw-Agent-Policy block in AGENTS.md
- `delivery.channel` — concrete (`mattermost` / `feishu` / …), never `last`
- `delivery.accountId` — `default` for main, else `agentId`
- `delivery.to` — `user:<peerId>` from the live session key

**End users never supply any of this.** A new silent **Step 0 — Session
Context Capture** runs at the top of every generated pack-onboarding
SKILL.md: it resolves `agent_id`, `account_id`, `channel`, and `peer_id`
from `AGENTS.md` + the session key and writes them to `data/config.json`.
Later cron creations substitute `{{config.*}}` placeholders — no user
prompt, ever. If an ID can't be resolved at capture time, onboarding
defers cron creation and logs a retry note to `MEMORY.md`. Asking the
user is never correct — they aren't engineers and have no concept of a
peerId or session key.

Changes:
- `references/automation.md`: cron JSON templates now show the full
  required shape (`enabled`, `agentId`, `wakeMode`, full `delivery` with
  `accountId`/`to`/`bestEffort`). Added Step 0 at the top of the
  onboarding step list. Updated the "immediately create cron" example
  inside the `write_onboarding`-must-embed-this section.
- `scripts/write_cron.py`: dropped the broken `channel: "last"` default;
  callers supply a populated delivery, and we render `accountId`/`to`
  when present.
- `scripts/write_onboarding.py`: generated SKILL.md now begins with the
  silent Step 0 capture, and each automation's cron-tool invocation uses
  `{{config.*}}` placeholders that the pack agent substitutes from
  `data/config.json` at call time.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* agent-studio: document platform-injected heartbeat delivery + reply-text convention

Mirrors ecap-workspace#770 (feat: inject per-agent heartbeat delivery
config). When a bot is deployed, the platform writes a `heartbeat` block
into each agent's entry in `agents.list`:

| Agent     | target      | accountId    | to                    |
|-----------|-------------|--------------|-----------------------|
| main      | mattermost  | _(omitted)_  | user:<mm_user_id>     |
| sub-agent | mattermost  | <agent_id>   | user:<mm_user_id>     |

Without this injection (pre-#770), heartbeat messages were silently
misdelivered in multi-bot Mattermost deployments.

The agent-side contract is: heartbeat firings carry the prompt "Output
your message as reply text (do NOT use message tool). If nothing to send:
reply HEARTBEAT_OK." The platform routes the reply text via the injected
config. Calling the `message` tool instead bypasses the heartbeat channel
and silently loses the notification.

Changes:
- `references/automation.md`: replaced the "notify user" line in the
  example HEARTBEAT.md with an explicit reply-text instruction. Added a
  paragraph documenting the platform-injected delivery config and the
  pack's minimal responsibility (keep the checklist + output reply text).
- `scripts/write_heartbeat.py`: the terminal "If action needed → notify
  user" line now tells the agent to output reply text and warns against
  the `message` tool. `HEARTBEAT_OK` is now explicitly backtick-wrapped
  for clarity.

Pack authors don't configure the heartbeat delivery — it's platform-
managed at bot-deploy time. They only ensure HEARTBEAT.md is correct and
that the agent emits reply text.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* simplify: fix timezone placeholder bug + render cron JSON as fenced block

Review pass found three real issues in the delivery-policy delta:

1. BUG — `{{config.timezone}}` placeholder in the generated cron JSON had
   no source: Step 0 captured only `agent_id` / `account_id` / `channel` /
   `peer_id`. Resolution would fail silently and produce an invalid
   schedule.tz at cron-add time. Fix: Step 0 now also captures `timezone`
   from `USER.md` (default `UTC` if missing) and writes it to
   `data/config.json`.

2. RENDERING — emitted cron JSON was a single long string wrapped in
   inline backticks; unreadable in the generated SKILL.md. Fix: emit via
   `json.dumps(..., indent=2)` inside a fenced ```json block, indented
   two spaces so it nests under the list item.

3. DEDUP — every automation repeated the same "if config.json is missing,
   re-run the session-context capture step" prose. Moved to Step 0 once;
   per-automation copy deleted.

Also tightened:
- Step 0 prose (verbose lead-in + triplicate "never ask user" emphasis
  collapsed to a single bold clause).
- `automation.md` heartbeat section (table + redundant summary paragraph
  collapsed to one inline sentence covering the same rules).
- `automation.md` cron delivery bullets: noted the one main-agent
  asymmetry vs heartbeat (cron requires `accountId: "default"`;
  heartbeat omits the field).
- `write_cron.py`: delivery comment trimmed from 4 lines to 2.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* write_cron.py: document that this is manifest-only, not runtime

Follow-up review noted ambiguity around write_cron.py's schema: it omits
`enabled` / `agentId` / `wakeMode` / `delivery.bestEffort` compared to the
cron-tool JSON emitted by write_onboarding.py. That looked like a bug at
first glance — but the `cron_jobs:` block in agent-pack.yaml is consumed
only by generate_description.py, which reads name/description for the
Agent Mart listing. It is NOT translated to cron-tool calls at runtime;
the actual scheduling happens via the full {{config.*}} JSON from
write_onboarding.py.

Adding a module docstring to prevent the same misreading next time.
Behavior unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* generate_description.py: detect cron jobs in multi-line onboarding JSON

Codex follow-up review caught a regression I introduced in commit 992b907
(which switched the emitted cron JSON in pack-onboarding SKILL.md from a
single backtick-wrapped line to a pretty-printed fenced block).

The existing detector in `generate_description.py` looked for "name",
"action", and "add" ALL on the same line and then regex-matched
`"name": "..."`. With pretty-printed JSON, those three tokens are now
each on their own line, so the listing's automations array came back
empty — users would see zero automations in the Agent Mart listing even
though the pack creates real cron jobs.

Fix: scan the onboarding SKILL.md content as a single string (using
re.DOTALL) for any `"action": "add"` / `"name": "..."` pair within the
same JSON block. The pattern matches both orderings (so the pack format
remains flexible) and still handles the legacy single-line form.

Verified end-to-end: a pack-onboarding SKILL.md with two pretty-printed
cron JSONs now surfaces both job names in the description.json
automations array.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* generate_description.py: parse each cron JSON block independently

Previous fix (d2ffe5d) used a single re.DOTALL regex over the whole
onboarding SKILL.md. Codex follow-up correctly pointed out two risks:

1. Cross-talk: the lazy `.*?` could pair an `"action": "add"` from one
   cron block with a `"name"` from an unrelated following block.
2. Prose false positives: any literal `"action": "add"` or `"name": "..."`
   occurring in a comment, doc, or message field would match.

Replaced with a proper two-step: (a) extract each fenced ```json block
(new form) AND each backtick-wrapped inline JSON string that mentions
"action" (legacy form), (b) json.loads each block independently and
only take names where `action == "add"`. Scoped per-block; no
cross-talk; prose containing the literal words but not valid JSON is
ignored.

Verified:
- multi-line fenced JSON, two jobs → both names extracted
- legacy single-line inline JSON → name extracted
- prose mentioning "action" / "add" outside a JSON block → ignored
- malformed JSON block between valid ones → skipped gracefully
- non-"add" actions (e.g. "list") → filtered out

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
agent-studio: simplify instructions (-36% words) + clean up lifecycle scripts (#89)

* agent-studio: simplify instructions + tighten lifecycle scripts

The Agent Studio skill is loaded every time a creator uses the `/dev` flow in
ZooClaw, so every token in SKILL.md / AGENTS.md / references costs real money
on each run. This PR shrinks the instruction surface ~36% while preserving
every rule, command, required field, and semantic, then cleans up a handful
of latent issues in the lifecycle scripts.

## MD simplification (-441 net lines)

Word-count deltas:

| File                  | Before | After | Δ        |
|-----------------------|--------|-------|----------|
| references/automation | 2381   | 1192  | -50%     |
| references/skill-design | 1752 | 965   | -45%     |
| references/data-sources | 833  | 523   | -37%     |
| SKILL.md              | 883    | 754   | -15%     |
| references/publishing | 439    | 359   | -18%     |
| AGENTS.md             | 418    | 358   | -14%     |
| references/discovery  | 391    | 340   | -13%     |
| references/testing    | 229    | 190   | -17%     |
| BOOTSTRAP.md          | 136    | 114   | -16%     |
| **Total**             | **7462** | **4795** | **-36%** |

At ~1.3 tokens/word that's ≈3.5k tokens saved per full skill invocation when
all references get loaded, plus ≈250 tokens saved on every trigger from the
always-loaded pair (SKILL.md + AGENTS.md).

What was cut:
- WRONG-example pairs (the CORRECT example teaches alone)
- The second of two parallel JSON examples
- Stage-2 process rules that were restated in both SKILL.md and skill-design.md
- Verbose prose that restated the adjacent table/code block
- Filler emphasis like "CRITICAL:" / "Key principles:" where structure was
  already obvious

All rules, commands, required-field lists, CLI auth cases, billing-mode
tables, and cron-tool JSON shape are preserved.

## Code changes

**New `scripts/_common.py`** — shared `extract_field` / `extract_multiline_field`
/ `extract_list_field` helpers. Deduplicates 3 copies of the first extractor
(publish.py + generate_description.py) and the `_extract_yaml_description`
variant in validate.py. The common multiline extractor also recognises all
four block-scalar indicators (`>`, `|`, `>-`, `|-`, `>+`, `|+`).

**publish.py**
- Fixes a real tmpdir leak: `tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix="publish_")` for agent-file
  staging and `tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix="publish_data_")` (one per data template)
  were never cleaned up, leaving `/tmp/publish_*` dirs around on every publish.
  Wrapped the whole build in a single `TemporaryDirectory` context so both are
  cleaned automatically, and pass the shared tmpdir into `_include_data_templates`.
- Removes unused `from io import BytesIO` import.
- Factors the duplicated avatar-location loop (steps 0 and 7) into `_find_avatar`.
- `_should_include`: `.git` and `.pytest_cache` in `dirs_to_skip` are now
  redundant with the new `part.startswith(".")` hidden-dir filter — dropped.
- Drops a no-op "ensure data/ directory exists" comment that described no code.

**validate.py**
- `check_agent_completeness` had a dict mapping each required file to `None`,
  only so the loop could test `if required_marker and ...` — that branch is
  unreachable (the value is always `None`). Simplified to a plain list.

**clean.py**
- Removes an `errors = []` local that was initialised, returned in JSON, but
  never written to.

**generate_description.py**
- Migrated to use `_common.*` extractors; local copies deleted.

## Verification

All 9 scripts compile (`python3 -m py_compile`). `validate.py --pack-dir .`
and `generate_description.py --pack-dir .` both run end-to-end on a blank
Stage-1 workspace and emit well-formed JSON.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* publish.py: reject absolute/traversal paths in data_templates

Codex review flagged that `_include_data_templates` joined `tmpl_path` from
the manifest without checking for absolute paths or `..` components. On a
malformed or hostile manifest, `full_path = pack_dir / tmpl_path` could read
outside the pack dir and `tmp_path = tmpdir / "data_templates" / tmpl_path`
could materialize outside the staging tmpdir. Practically unreachable since
creators are trusted and scaffolded manifests use clean relative paths, but
cheap to harden: skip any template whose path is absolute or contains `..`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* publish.py: also reject symlinks that escape pack_dir

Codex follow-up review noted the previous guard only caught lexical escapes
(absolute paths / `..`). A manifest entry `"path": "evil"` where `evil` is a
symlink inside pack_dir pointing to `/etc/passwd` still passes the string
check, and `full_path.exists()` follows the link so `tar.add` would read the
target.

Resolve the full path and refuse anything whose resolved form sits outside
pack_dir. Legitimate in-pack files resolve within; symlinks-to-outside raise
`ValueError` on `relative_to` and are skipped.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* publish.py: extend symlink-escape check to agent/ files and avatar

Follow-up to previous commit: Codex consistency check noted that once
data_templates were guarded, the agent/ iteration and avatar lookup needed
the same treatment — both follow symlinks via `_clean_agent_file`'s
`read_text()` / `shutil.copy2()`.

- `_should_include`: resolve the path and skip if it falls outside base_dir.
  This also covers the skills/ iteration, which uses the same filter.
- `_find_avatar`: apply the same resolve-and-check before returning.

Creators are trusted and this was a pre-existing behavior, but the guards
are cheap and remove ambiguity under any future threat-model change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* publish.py: factor symlink-escape check, guard all remaining reads

Codex pointed out that `manifest_path.read_text()` at the top of main()
wasn't guarded — same class as the earlier findings. Rather than whack-a-mole
every read, extract a tiny `_within_pack(path, pack_root)` helper and apply
it consistently at every read site: main()'s manifest load, _generate_summary's
SKILL.md + manifest rereads, _clean_agent_file's data/config.json read, and
_should_include (already covered). _find_avatar now uses the helper too.

Net: every disk read in publish.py is now symlink-safe with respect to
pack_dir. Still a belt-and-braces defence (creators are trusted), but no
longer has inconsistent coverage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

variant in validate.py. The common multiline extractor also recognises all
four block-scalar indicators (`>`, `|`, `>-`, `|-`, `>+`, `|+`).

**publish.py**
- Fixes a real tmpdir leak: `tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix="publish_")` for agent-file
  staging and `tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix="publish_data_")` (one per data template)
  were never cleaned up, leaving `/tmp/publish_*` dirs around on every publish.
  Wrapped the whole build in a single `TemporaryDirectory` context so both are
  cleaned automatically, and pass the shared tmpdir into `_include_data_templates`.
- Removes unused `from io import BytesIO` import.
- Factors the duplicated avatar-location loop (steps 0 and 7) into `_find_avatar`.
- `_should_include`: `.git` and `.pytest_cache` in `dirs_to_skip` are now
  redundant with the new `part.startswith(".")` hidden-dir filter — dropped.
- Drops a no-op "ensure data/ directory exists" comment that described no code.

**validate.py**
- `check_agent_completeness` had a dict mapping each required file to `None`,
  only so the loop could test `if required_marker and ...` — that branch is
  unreachable (the value is always `None`). Simplified to a plain list.

**clean.py**
- Removes an `errors = []` local that was initialised, returned in JSON, but
  never written to.

**generate_description.py**
- Migrated to use `_common.*` extractors; local copies deleted.

## Verification

All 9 scripts compile (`python3 -m py_compile`). `validate.py --pack-dir .`
and `generate_description.py --pack-dir .` both run end-to-end on a blank
Stage-1 workspace and emit well-formed JSON.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* publish.py: reject absolute/traversal paths in data_templates

Codex review flagged that `_include_data_templates` joined `tmpl_path` from
the manifest without checking for absolute paths or `..` components. On a
malformed or hostile manifest, `full_path = pack_dir / tmpl_path` could read
outside the pack dir and `tmp_path = tmpdir / "data_templates" / tmpl_path`
could materialize outside the staging tmpdir. Practically unreachable since
creators are trusted and scaffolded manifests use clean relative paths, but
cheap to harden: skip any template whose path is absolute or contains `..`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* publish.py: also reject symlinks that escape pack_dir

Codex follow-up review noted the previous guard only caught lexical escapes
(absolute paths / `..`). A manifest entry `"path": "evil"` where `evil` is a
symlink inside pack_dir pointing to `/etc/passwd` still passes the string
check, and `full_path.exists()` follows the link so `tar.add` would read the
target.

Resolve the full path and refuse anything whose resolved form sits outside
pack_dir. Legitimate in-pack files resolve within; symlinks-to-outside raise
`ValueError` on `relative_to` and are skipped.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* publish.py: extend symlink-escape check to agent/ files and avatar

Follow-up to previous commit: Codex consistency check noted that once
data_templates were guarded, the agent/ iteration and avatar lookup needed
the same treatment — both follow symlinks via `_clean_agent_file`'s
`read_text()` / `shutil.copy2()`.

- `_should_include`: resolve the path and skip if it falls outside base_dir.
  This also covers the skills/ iteration, which uses the same filter.
- `_find_avatar`: apply the same resolve-and-check before returning.

Creators are trusted and this was a pre-existing behavior, but the guards
are cheap and remove ambiguity under any future threat-model change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* publish.py: factor symlink-escape check, guard all remaining reads

Codex pointed out that `manifest_path.read_text()` at the top of main()
wasn't guarded — same class as the earlier findings. Rather than whack-a-mole
every read, extract a tiny `_within_pack(path, pack_root)` helper and apply
it consistently at every read site: main()'s manifest load, _generate_summary's
SKILL.md + manifest rereads, _clean_agent_file's data/config.json read, and
_should_include (already covered). _find_avatar now uses the helper too.

Net: every disk read in publish.py is now symlink-safe with respect to
pack_dir. Still a belt-and-braces defence (creators are trusted), but no
longer has inconsistent coverage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: move agent-studio to .agents/skills/, pack skills under .agents/skills/, avatar & hidden-dir fixes (#79)

* refactor: move agent-studio skill to .agents/skills/ and pack skills under .agents/skills/

OpenClaw natively supports loading skills from {workspace}/.agents/skills/ (project-level
agents skills directory). This change separates the agent-studio dev tooling from the pack's
own skills directory, making packaging cleaner:

- Dev mode: agent-studio lives in .agents/skills/agent-studio/ (hidden, dev-only)
- Dev mode: new agent skills are created in skills/ as before
- Packaging: publish.py now packs skills/ contents into .agents/skills/ in the archive
- Removed all agent-studio exclusion logic from publish.py, validate.py, clean.py,
  and generate_description.py since agent-studio is no longer in skills/
- Updated all path references in AGENTS.md, SKILL.md, design docs, and 30+ test files
- Restored tests and design docs that were removed upstream (needed for this project)
- All 223 tests pass

commit.message:
fix: backward compat for /extra-skills mount on older pods (#87)

* fix: add backward compat for /extra-skills mount on older pods

PR #84/#85/#86 replaced /extra-skills paths with ~/.openclaw/skills
(following fastclaw#56), but ~395 of ~431 bot pods are still running
older images without the new mount point. This causes ENOENT when
agents are redeployed on these pods.

Changes:
- Python scripts: detect ~/.openclaw/skills at import time, fall back
  to /extra-skills if it does not exist
- SKILL.md bash blocks: prepend SKILLS=... shell variable with the
  same detection logic
- generate_collage.py: add _parse_designer_output() to handle both
  old (JSON) and new (plain path) designer CLI stdout formats
- Designer CLI flag compat: use IMGFLAG variable (--images vs
  --image-urls) tied to the detected mount point

Affected agents: stylist-agent, restaurant-review-monitor,
zoodance-vibe-drama

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* simplify: remove IMGFLAG — skills content is identical across mount points

The old (/extra-skills) and new (~/.openclaw/skills) mounts contain
the same code; only the path differs. No need for CLI flag or stdout
format compat — just path detection is sufficient.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: soulmate session cooldown detection was completely broken (#74)

Three bugs in get_session_updated_at() caused active session cooldown
to never work:

1. Missing --agent flag: `openclaw sessions --json` only lists the
   default agent's sessions, not soulmate's.
2. Wrong field name: checked `sessionKey` but the actual JSON field
   is `key`.
3. Timeout too short: openclaw CLI takes >10s on pod, causing
   TimeoutExpired to be silently swallowed by the bare except.
4. Stdout pollution: openclaw prints plugin registration logs to
   stdout after JSON, causing json.loads to fail with Extra data.

All four issues made get_session_updated_at() always return None,
so the bot could send proactive messages even during active conversations.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
